speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Great lords and gentlemen, what means this silence?

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Dare no man answer in a case of truth?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Within the Temple Hall we were too loud;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The garden here is more convenient.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Then say at once if I maintained the truth,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Or else was wrangling Somerset in th’ error?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Faith, I have been a truant in the law

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And never yet could frame my will to it,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And therefore frame the law unto my will.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Judge you, my Lord of Warwick, then, between us.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Between two hawks, which flies the higher pitch,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Between two dogs, which hath the deeper mouth,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Between two blades, which bears the better temper,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Between two horses, which doth bear him best,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Between two girls, which hath the merriest eye,

speaker: WARWICK
text: I have perhaps some shallow spirit of judgment;

speaker: WARWICK
text: But in these nice sharp quillets of the law,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Good faith, I am no wiser than a daw.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Tut, tut, here is a mannerly forbearance!

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: The truth appears so naked on my side

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: That any purblind eye may find it out.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: And on my side it is so well appareled,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: So clear, so shining, and so evident,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: That it will glimmer through a blind man’s eye.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Since you are tongue-tied and so loath to speak,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: In dumb significants proclaim your thoughts:

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Let him that is a trueborn gentleman

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: And stands upon the honor of his birth,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: If he suppose that I have pleaded truth,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: From off this brier pluck a white rose with me.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Let him that is no coward nor no flatterer,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: But dare maintain the party of the truth,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Pluck a red rose from off this thorn with me.

speaker: WARWICK
text: I love no colors; and, without all color

speaker: WARWICK
text: Of base insinuating flattery,

speaker: WARWICK
text: I pluck this white rose with Plantagenet.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I pluck this red rose with young Somerset,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And say withal I think he held the right.

speaker: VERNON
text: Stay, lords and gentlemen, and pluck no more

speaker: VERNON
text: Till you conclude that he upon whose side

speaker: VERNON
text: The fewest roses are croppèd from the tree

speaker: VERNON
text: Shall yield the other in the right opinion.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Good Master Vernon, it is well objected:

speaker: SOMERSET
text: If I have fewest, I subscribe in silence.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: And I.

speaker: VERNON
text: Then for the truth and plainness of the case,

speaker: VERNON
text: I pluck this pale and maiden blossom here,

speaker: VERNON
text: Giving my verdict on the white rose side.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Prick not your finger as you pluck it off,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Lest, bleeding, you do paint the white rose red,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: And fall on my side so against your will.

speaker: VERNON
text: If I, my lord, for my opinion bleed,

speaker: VERNON
text: Opinion shall be surgeon to my hurt

speaker: VERNON
text: And keep me on the side where still I am.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Well, well, come on, who else?

speaker: LAWYER
text: Unless my study and my books be false,

speaker: LAWYER
text: The argument you held was wrong in law,

speaker: LAWYER
text: In sign whereof I pluck a white rose too.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Now, Somerset, where is your argument?

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Here in my scabbard, meditating that

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Shall dye your white rose in a bloody red.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Meantime your cheeks do counterfeit our roses,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: For pale they look with fear, as witnessing

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: The truth on our side.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: No, Plantagenet.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: ’Tis not for fear, but anger that thy cheeks

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Blush for pure shame to counterfeit our roses,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: And yet thy tongue will not confess thy error.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Hath not thy rose a canker, Somerset?

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Hath not thy rose a thorn, Plantagenet?

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Ay, sharp and piercing, to maintain his truth,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Whiles thy consuming canker eats his falsehood.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Well, I’ll find friends to wear my bleeding roses

speaker: SOMERSET
text: That shall maintain what I have said is true,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Where false Plantagenet dare not be seen.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Now, by this maiden blossom in my hand,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: I scorn thee and thy fashion, peevish boy.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Turn not thy scorns this way, Plantagenet.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Proud Pole, I will, and scorn both him and thee.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I’ll turn my part thereof into thy throat.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Away, away, good William de la Pole!

speaker: SOMERSET
text: We grace the yeoman by conversing with him.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Now, by God’s will, thou wrong’st him, Somerset.

speaker: WARWICK
text: His grandfather was Lionel, Duke of Clarence,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Third son to the third Edward, King of England.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Spring crestless yeomen from so deep a root?

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: He bears him on the place’s privilege,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Or durst not for his craven heart say thus.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: By Him that made me, I’ll maintain my words

speaker: SOMERSET
text: On any plot of ground in Christendom.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Was not thy father Richard, Earl of Cambridge,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: For treason executed in our late king’s days?

speaker: SOMERSET
text: And, by his treason, stand’st not thou attainted,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Corrupted, and exempt from ancient gentry?

speaker: SOMERSET
text: His trespass yet lives guilty in thy blood,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: And, till thou be restored, thou art a yeoman.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: My father was attachèd, not attainted,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Condemned to die for treason, but no traitor;

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: And that I’ll prove on better men than Somerset,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Were growing time once ripened to my will.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: For your partaker Pole and you yourself,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: I’ll note you in my book of memory

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: To scourge you for this apprehension.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Look to it well, and say you are well warned.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Ah, thou shalt find us ready for thee still,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: And know us by these colors for thy foes,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: For these my friends in spite of thee shall wear.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: And, by my soul, this pale and angry rose,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: As cognizance of my blood-drinking hate,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Will I forever, and my faction, wear

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Until it wither with me to my grave

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Or flourish to the height of my degree.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Go forward, and be choked with thy ambition!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And so farewell, until I meet thee next.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Have with thee, Pole.—Farewell, ambitious Richard.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: How I am braved, and must perforce endure it!

speaker: WARWICK
text: This blot that they object against your house

speaker: WARWICK
text: Shall be whipped out in the next parliament,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Called for the truce of Winchester and Gloucester;

speaker: WARWICK
text: And if thou be not then created York,

speaker: WARWICK
text: I will not live to be accounted Warwick.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Meantime, in signal of my love to thee,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Against proud Somerset and William Pole

speaker: WARWICK
text: Will I upon thy party wear this rose.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And here I prophesy: this brawl today,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Grown to this faction in the Temple garden,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Shall send, between the red rose and the white,

speaker: WARWICK
text: A thousand souls to death and deadly night.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Good Master Vernon, I am bound to you,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: That you on my behalf would pluck a flower.

speaker: VERNON
text: In your behalf still will I wear the same.

speaker: LAWYER
text: And so will I.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Thanks, gentle sir.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Come, let us four to dinner. I dare say

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: This quarrel will drink blood another day.
